task_objective: Implement backend validation for RPC-exposed process signaling PIDs so unsafe PID values cannot reach os.kill.
design_decisions: Added a backend-only _coerce_signal_pid helper near the process signaling helpers.
Rejected bool, float, malformed string, blank string, and PID values less than or equal to 1 before any signal dispatch.
Rejected PID values above 2147483647 before dispatch so oversized Python integers cannot reach os.kill pid_t conversion.
Parsed string PIDs with int(cleaned), not isdigit or isdecimal, so signed strings are handled consistently.
Handled ValueError locally in pause_game_process and resume_game_process to return structured failure results and warning logs without Plugin._call exception traces.
Kept public RPC names, TypeScript types, process-tree traversal, frontend lifecycle flow, and dependencies unchanged.

results.red_tests: Targeted new tests failed before implementation because unsafe PIDs still signaled and _coerce_signal_pid did not exist.
full_validation: ./run.sh uv run ruff format . -> 50 files left unchanged
./run.sh uv run ty check py_modules/sdh_ludusavi/ -> passed
